title: 10 best herbal teas for excessive sweating
intro_desc: Herbal teas for excessive sweating are infusions made from various dried herbs, flowers, and spices that can help regulate perspiration. These teas are caffeine-free and often contain natural ingredients known for their calming, diuretic, or antiperspirant properties. Examples include chamomile, known for its calming effects; sage, which has antiperspirant properties; and peppermint, which can help regulate body temperature. By drinking these teas regularly, individuals may experience a reduction in excessive sweating, leading to improved confidence, comfort, and overall quality of life. For instance, wearing certain clothes or participating in social activities without the fear of sweating excessively can significantly boost one's self-esteem and well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow teas helps with excessive sweating because it contains a compound called apigenin, which has natural antiperspirant properties. This compound helps to reduce sweat production by binding to the eccrine glands in the skin, preventing them from overactivating and releasing excess moisture. Additionally, yarrow tea's cooling and soothing effects can help to calm the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety that may also contribute to excessive sweating. As a result, drinking yarrow tea can provide relief from clammy hands, feet, and face.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic: Yarrow tea helps to relax muscle spasms, which can contribute to excessive sweating by reducing the body's natural response to stress and anxiety.

Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain caused by excessive sweating, making it a great remedy for athletes or individuals who experience intense physical activity.
Antimicrobial: Yarrow tea contains antimicrobial compounds that can help combat fungal and bacterial infections that may exacerbate excessive sweating, such as hyperhidrosis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which have a cooling effect on the body, helping to reduce excessive sweating.
Bark: Containing salicin and other phenolic compounds, it helps to reduce inflammation and soothe the skin associated with excessive sweating.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried yarrow leaves and flowers according to your preferred method.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried yarrow herb for every eight ounces of water needed.
Heat filtered water in a teapot or kettle to boiling point before pouring over the herbs.
Allow the herbal infusion to steep for 5-7 minutes before straining and serving hot or cold.
Store any leftover tea in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to three days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood teas helps with excessive sweating because of its natural ability to balance the body's sweat glands. The herbal remedy contains thujone, a compound that targets the hypothalamus, the part of the brain that regulates body temperature and sweat production. By reducing the activity of this gland, wormwood tea can help to normalize sweat levels and alleviate symptoms of hyperhidrosis, providing relief from excessive sweating and associated discomfort.
remedy_properties: Thermogenic and diaphoretic properties: Wormwood tea helps stimulate sweat glands to release heat from the body, providing relief from excessive sweating.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects: Wormwood's antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with hyperhidrosis (excessive sweating), which can cause discomfort and skin issues.

Astringent and antiseptic properties: Wormwood tea's astringent properties help constrict sweat glands, reducing the amount of sweat produced, while its antiseptic properties prevent bacterial growth and promote healthy skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing sesquiterpenes, which help to reduce sweat glands' activity and alleviate hyperhidrosis symptoms.
Seeds: Rich in alkaloids, such as absinthin, which have a drying effect on the skin and can help to reduce sweating.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried artemisia absinthium leaves and flowers in quantities of approximately one ounce.
Rinse the herbs gently with cold filtered water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine the rinsed herbs with eight ounces of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass teapot.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes, then strain through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a separate container.
Allow the tea to cool and settle for at least one hour before serving.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Silver birch teas helps with excessive sweating because it contains a unique combination of flavonoids, terpenes, and polyphenols that have potent antiperspirant properties. The tea's active compounds help to reduce sweat production by blocking the activity of neurotransmitters that stimulate sweat glands, resulting in a significant decrease in perspiration. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of silver birch tea may also help to soothe and calm irritated skin, further alleviating symptoms associated with excessive sweating.
remedy_properties: Antipyretic property: Silver birch tea has antipyretic properties, which means it helps to reduce body temperature and alleviate fever-like symptoms, including excessive sweating caused by heat or inflammation.

Diuretic property: The diuretic properties of silver birch tea help to increase urine production, which can help to remove excess toxins and fluids from the body that may contribute to excessive sweating.
Anti-inflammatory property: Silver birch tea has anti-inflammatory compounds that can help to reduce inflammation in the body, which is a common underlying cause of excessive sweating.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Silver Birch plant have been traditionally used to treat excessive sweating due to their diaphoretic properties, which help to increase perspiration and promote detoxification.
Bark: The bark of the Silver Birch tree has been used to create a tea that helps to reduce sweating by soothing the nervous system and calming the body's natural response to heat.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried silver birch leaves and flowers according to seasonal availability.
Combine 2 teaspoons of chopped dried silver birch leaves with 250ml boiling water for infusion.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes then strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup.
Add one tablespoon of honey to taste and stir well to dissolve the sweetener completely.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before serving warm or refrigerating for later consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White tea teas helps with excessive sweating because of its unique composition of antioxidants, flavonoids, and catechins. These compounds work together to calm the body's temperature regulation system, reducing the production of heat-stimulating hormones that can cause excessive sweating. Additionally, white tea's gentle astringent properties help to tighten pores and absorb moisture, further reducing sweat buildup and leaving skin feeling dry and refreshed.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: White tea's high antioxidant content helps to neutralize free radicals, which contribute to oxidative stress and inflammation, both of which can exacerbate excessive sweating.
Astringent compounds: Herbal white teas contain astringent compounds like tannins and flavonoids that help to constrict sweat glands, reducing the amount of sweat produced and improving skin tone.

Anti-inflammatory agents: White tea's anti-inflammatory properties, particularly those found in its catechin content, can help to reduce inflammation and irritation associated with excessive sweating, such as skin redness and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and flavonoids, which help to regulate body temperature and reduce sweat production.
Bark: Containing tannins and polyphenols, it helps to tighten pores and reduce excessive sweating by contracting the skin's sweat glands.
remedy_recipe: Select high-quality camellia sinensis leaves for white tea brewing.
Measure 1 teaspoon of loose-leaf white tea per 8 ounces of filtered water.
Heat filtered water to a temperature between 150°f and 160°f for steeping.
Steep the white tea leaves for 2-3 minutes in the heated water mixture.
Strain the brewed tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or tea strainer.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Roman chamomile teas helps with excessive sweating because of its calming and soothing properties, which help to reduce anxiety and stress. Excessive sweating is often linked to emotional states such as nervousness and anxiety, and chamomile's natural tranquilizing effects can help to alleviate these feelings. Additionally, the tea's anti-inflammatory properties can also help to soothe the skin and reduce sweating, making it a popular remedy for people who suffer from excessive perspiration.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Roman Chamomile tea helps to relax muscles, which can alleviate sweaty palms and soles caused by stress or anxiety.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in Roman Chamomile tea can reduce inflammation and irritation in the skin, which may help to alleviate excessive sweating caused by conditions such as hyperhidrosis.
Sedative properties: The calming effects of Roman Chamomile tea can help to reduce stress and anxiety, common triggers for excessive sweating.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in apigenin and other flavonoids, roots help calm the nervous system and reduce perspiration.
Flowers: Containing bisabolol and other sesquiterpenes, flowers soothe and relax the body, reducing sweat production.
remedy_recipe: Measure one tablespoon of dried roman chamomile flowers per eight ounces of water.
Heat filtered water to a simmer in a saucepan or teapot.
Steep the chamomile flowers for five to seven minutes allowing the flavors to meld together.
Strain the tea into a cup using a strainer or cheesecloth to catch any loose particles.
Enjoy the soothing and calming effects of the herbal tea after it has cooled to a comfortable temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eureka teas helps with excessive sweating because it contains a unique blend of herbs that work synergistically to calm the nervous system, reduce stress hormones, and regulate body temperature. The soothing properties of chamomile, lemon balm, and peppermint help to ease anxiety and promote relaxation, while the antiperspirant properties of witch hazel and sage help to constrict sweat glands and reduce excessive moisture. This holistic approach can bring relief from hot flashes, night sweats, and hyperhidrosis, allowing individuals to feel cooler, calmer, and more confident throughout their day.
remedy_properties: Antiperspirant properties: Eureka Tea's antiperspirant properties help to reduce sweat production by blocking the sweat glands, providing relief from excessive sweating and body odor.
Cooling effect: The cooling effect of Eureka Tea helps to calm the skin and reduce inflammation caused by excessive sweating, making it an effective remedy for hyperhidrosis (excessive sweating).
Antioxidant properties: Antioxidants in Eureka Tea help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress and inflammation, which can exacerbate excessive sweating and its symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in compounds that help to reduce sweat glands' activity, calming excessive sweating.
Rhyzomes: Contains anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the skin, reducing sweat-related discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh eureka lemon leaves from your garden or purchase them from local suppliers weighing about 20-25 grams.
Rinse the harvested leaves thoroughly with cool running water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine the rinsed leaves with 500 milliliters of filtered boiling water in a large glass jar or teapot allowing it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into another container discarding the solids and preserving the liquid for consumption.
Chill the strained tea in the refrigerator for at least 2 hours or serve immediately over ice to enjoy the refreshing citrus flavor.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horsetail teas helps with excessive sweating because it contains high levels of silicon, a mineral that has been shown to help reduce sweat production. Silicon also improves the strength and elasticity of skin, which can help to reduce the appearance of moisture-loving pores, thereby reducing excessive sweating. Additionally, horsetail's anti-inflammatory properties may also help to soothe and calm irritated skin, further contributing to its ability to alleviate sweaty palms, feet, and other areas prone to excessive perspiration.
remedy_properties: Silica content: Horsetail tea is rich in silica, which helps to improve skin elasticity and strength, reducing sweat glands' ability to produce excess moisture.
Anti-inflammatory compounds: The anti-inflammatory properties in horsetail tea may help reduce inflammation caused by excessive sweating, leading to a decrease in sweat production.
Antioxidants: Horsetail tea's antioxidants can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to excessive sweating and related skin issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in silicon and other minerals, the roots help to strengthen connective tissue and improve skin health, reducing excessive sweating.
Rhyzomes: The underground stems of the plant contain high amounts of silica, which helps to stimulate sweat glands and reduce excessive sweating.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried horsetail leaves and stems according to local regulations and sustainable practices.
Rinse 1 tablespoon of dried horsetail or 2 tablespoons of fresh horsetail with cool water to remove impurities.
Combine the rinsed horsetail with 8 ounces of boiling water in a teapot or tea infuser and let steep for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup, discarding the solids and retaining the liquid.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before consuming it immediately or storing it in the refrigerator for up to 2 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel teas helps with excessive sweating because of its natural astringent properties, which help to dry up excessive moisture in the body. The tea's active compounds, including flavonoids and coumarins, also have a cooling effect that can help to reduce heat and sweat production. Additionally, fennel's antifungal and antibacterial properties can help to prevent infections caused by sweat buildup, making it an effective natural remedy for excessive sweating.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Fennel tea's antimicrobial properties help to reduce the growth of bacteria on the skin, which can contribute to excessive sweating and body odor.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in fennel tea may help to reduce inflammation and irritation associated with excessive sweating, promoting a cooler and more comfortable body temperature.

Antioxidant properties: Fennel tea's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress and damage caused by excessive sweating, leading to improved overall skin health and reduced sweat production.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antiperspirant properties, fennel roots help regulate sweat glands and reduce excessive perspiration.
Seeds: Containing volatile oils, fennel seeds provide natural astringent and anti-inflammatory effects, calming the skin and reducing sweat.
Bark: With its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic compounds, fennel bark helps to balance body temperature and alleviate excessive sweating.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh fennel leaves or use dried ones in the ratio of 2 tablespoons per liter of water.
Chop the fresh fennel leaves into small pieces or crush the dried ones to release their oils.
Combine the chopped or crushed fennel with boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup or teapot and discard solids.
Add honey to taste if desired and serve hot, allowing the flavors to mingle for 2-3 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice teas helps with excessive sweating because of its unique composition, which includes glycyrrhizin and flavonoids. These compounds have natural antiperspirant properties that help to reduce sweat production by blocking the sweat glands' ability to produce perspiration. Additionally, licorice tea's anti-inflammatory agents may also soothe irritated skin, reducing redness and discomfort often associated with excessive sweating.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin, a key compound found in licorice root, helps to reduce sweat production by inhibiting the activity of enzymes involved in sweating, thereby providing relief from excessive sweating.

Flavonoids and triterpenoids: The flavonoids and triterpenoids present in licorice tea have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to calm the nervous system and reduce sweat production associated with anxiety or stress-induced hyperhidrosis.

Glycyrrhetic acid: Glycyrrhetic acid, a metabolite of glycyrrhizin, has been shown to exhibit antiperspirant activity by inhibiting the sweat glands' ability to release sweat, providing additional relief from excessive sweating.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant are used to make medicinal teas as they contain a high concentration of glycyrrhizin, which has been shown to reduce sweat and alleviate hot flashes associated with menopause.
Bark: The bark of the licorice plant is used to make medicinal teas due to its ability to calm the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress that can trigger excessive sweating.
remedy_recipe: Measure out one teaspoon of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots per 8 ounces water.
Bring the measured water to a boil in a medium-sized saucepan.
Add the dried licorice root mixture and reduce heat to a gentle simmer.
Allow the tea to steep for 5-7 minutes or until desired flavor is reached.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English ivy teas helps with excessive sweating because it has a natural ability to regulate body temperature. The herb contains flavonoids, which have a cooling effect on the skin, reducing heat and moisture retention. Additionally, English ivy's antispasmodic properties help to relax sweat glands, decreasing their activity and production of excess sweat. By drinking English ivy tea regularly, individuals can experience a significant reduction in excessive sweating, finding relief from this uncomfortable and embarrassing condition.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: English Ivy tea helps to relax muscles and reduce muscle spasms, which can contribute to excessive sweating by calming down overactive sweat glands.

Anti-inflammatory properties: English Ivy tea has anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the body, which may alleviate symptoms of excessive sweating such as redness and itching.

Antioxidant properties: English Ivy tea contains antioxidants that can neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to excessive sweating by reducing inflammation and promoting overall health.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and flavonoids, roots help reduce sweat production by calming the nervous system and regulating body temperature.
Rhyzomes: Containing essential oils and resins, rhyzomes work as a natural antiperspirant, reducing sweat glands' activity and preventing excessive sweating.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh english ivy leaves and rinse them thoroughly under cold running water.
Combine one teaspoon of dried or two teaspoons of fresh english ivy leaves with one cup boiling water.
Steep the mixture for seven to ten minutes then strain it through a fine-mesh sieve into a teapot.
Add honey or sugar to taste and stir well to dissolve sweetener if desired.
Serve the tea hot or allow it to cool and refrigerate for later consumption within 24 hours.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat excessive sweating with herbal teas is by regularly consuming sage and chamomile teas. These teas have antiperspirant and relaxant properties that help regulate sweat glands and reduce anxiety, a common cause of excessive sweating. Steep one teaspoon of dried herbs in boiling water for 10 minutes, strain and drink up to three cups a day. Remember, while herbal teas can be beneficial, it's important to consult with a healthcare professional for persistent or severe sweating issues.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to excessive sweating that are treated with herbal teas include anxiety and stress. Chamomile tea is known for its calming properties and can help reduce anxiety, which can in turn reduce excessive sweating. Peppermint tea is another herbal tea that can help alleviate symptoms of excessive sweating as it has a cooling effect on the body and can help regulate body temperature. Additionally, ginger tea can help boost the immune system and reduce inflammation, which can also help reduce sweating. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare professional before starting any new treatment regimen.
